chapter_title.en: Chapter on Trade, Its Etiquettes, Virtues, and Jurisprudence

text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ آخُذُ اَلدَّرَاهِمَ مِنَ اَلرَّجُلِ فَأَزِنُهَا ثُمَّ أُفَرِّقُهَا وَ يَفْضُلُ فِي يَدِي مِنْهَا فَضْلٌ قَالَ "أَ لَيْسَ تَحَرَّى اَلْوَفَاءَ" قُلْتُ بَلَى قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3749 - It is narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar who said:
I said to Abu Abdillah (as), "I receive dirhams from a man, weigh them, then distribute them, and a surplus remains in my hand. What should I do?"
Imam (as) said: "Did you not strive to be accurate in your measure?"
I said: "Yes."
Imam (as) said: "Then there is no harm."
